  • Überblick
  • Baum
  • Index
  • Suchen
  • Hilfe

    Alle Klassen und Schnittstellen

    Klasse
    Beschreibung
    AppConfig
    Root configuration class for the application.
    AppConfig.CryptanalysisConfig
     
    AppConfig.Data
     
    AppConfig.General
     
    AppConfig.GeneticAlgorithm
     
    AppConfig.KnapsackConfig
     
    AppConfig.PersistenceConfig
     
    AppConfig.Strategy
     
    AppConfig.SudokuConfig
     
    AppConfig.SymRegConfig
     
    AppConfig.TSPConfig
     
    BinaryFitnessFunction
     
    BitFlipMutation
    Description: Iterates through each gene in a binary sequence and tosses a coin based on the mutation rate.
    Chromosome<T>
    Represents a chromosome used in a genetic algorithm.
    ChromosomePair<T>
    A simple container holding two chromosomes.
    CipherDataService
    Processes CSV-based cipher data and converts it into a population of Individual objects for use in evolutionary algorithms.
    ConfigLoader
    Utility class responsible for loading the application configuration.
    ConfigurationException
    Exception thrown when invalid configuration values are provided in config.toml.
    ConsoleOutput
    Provides formatted console output for informational, warning, and error messages, including optional ANSI color codes for improved readability.
    ControllerFactory
    ControllerFactory dynamically generates a unified GAController based on the active configuration settings.
    Crossover<T>
    Contract for mixing the genetic data between two selected parent chromosomes.
    CryptanalysisFitnessFunction
    Fitness Function for the Cryptanalysis problem.
    CryptanalysisGAConfig
    Configuration class for setting up a genetic algorithm (GA) used for classical cipher cryptanalysis.
    CryptanalysisProblem
    Implementation of the Cryptanalysis problem for the Genetic Algorithm.
    CsvDataService
    Converts raw CSV rows into individuals for use in evolutionary algorithms.
    CsvReader
    A DataReader implementation for importing data from CSV files.
    CycleCrossover<T>
    Description: Identifies cycles between two parent chromosomes and swaps the genes within those cycles.
    DataReader<T>
    Generic interface for importing external data into the system.
    DataService<T>
    Defines a service for transforming raw CSV input data into a list of Individual objects suitable for use in evolutionary algorithms.
    FitnessFunction<T>
    Contract for evaluating the mathematical quality of a given candidate solution.
    GAController<T>
    Universal Controller encapsulating the execution context of a Genetic Algorithm cycle.
    GAProblem<T>
    Interface representing a generic Genetic Algorithm Problem Domain.
    GeneticAlgorithm<T>
    Implements the main workflow of a genetic algorithm.
    GeneticAlgorithmErrorTexts
    Contains predefined error and warning messages related to the configuration and execution of the genetic algorithm.
    Individual<T>
    Represents an individual in a genetic algorithm.
    Item
    Represents an item in the 0/1 knapsack problem, defined by a name, a weight, and a value.
    KnapsackFitnessFunction
    Fitness Function for the Knapsack problem.
    KnapsackGAConfig
    Configuration class for setting up a genetic algorithm (GA) to solve the 0/1 knapsack optimization problem.
    KnapsackProblem
    Implementation of the Knapsack problem for the Genetic Algorithm.
    LanguageProfile
    Data class representing a linguistic profile for the Cryptanalysis problem.
    LanguageRegistry
    Registry to manage available LanguageProfiles for Cryptanalysis.
    LinearRankingSelection<T>
    Description: A selection operator that ranks individuals by fitness and assigns selection probabilities linearly based on their rank rather than their raw fitness value.
    Main
    Entry point of the Integrea application.
    Mutation<T>
    Contract for inducing random genetic alterations into a given chromosome.
    OnePointCrossover<T>
    Description: Randomly selects a single division point along the genome length.
    OrderedCrossover<T>
    Description: Extracts a contiguous sub-sequence of genes from one parent and maps them directly into the offspring at the exact same index.
    PlainTextReader
    A DataReader implementation for importing plain text files.
    PointAndSubtreeMutation
    Description: A composite operator that randomly toggles (50/50 chance) between executing a delicate PointMutation (tweaking a single value) or a destructive SubtreeMutation (grafting a brand new random branch).
    PointMutation
    Description: Traverses an AST to pick a single node and perturbs it without altering the underlying tree structure.
    Population<T>
    Represents a population of individuals in a genetic algorithm.
    RouletteSelection<T>
    Description: A selection operator that simulates spinning a roulette wheel where the size of each "slice" is proportional to an individual's raw fitness score.
    Selection<T>
    Contract for selecting an individual from a population to act as a parent for the next generation.
    SimpleMutation<T>
    Description: Iterates over the genome and entirely replaces genes with random values fetched from a predefined range.
    StatePersistenceService
     
    StatePersistenceService.GaState
     
    StochasticUniversalSelection<T>
    Description: A selection operator akin to Roulette Wheel selection, but it utilizes multiple evenly spaced pointers to select parents.
    SubtreeCrossover
    Description: Simulates biological crossover by picking a random internal node from both parents and physically swapping the entire trailing sub-branches.
    SubtreeMutation
    Description: Randomly selects a node within a tree and completely replaces it (and its children) with a brand new, randomly generated subtree.
    SudokuBlockCrossover
    Description: An inherently 2-dimensional crossover mechanic.
    SudokuFitnessFunction
    Fitness Function for evaluating Sudoku grids.
    SudokuGAConfig
     
    SudokuHybridMutation
    Description: A dual-action operator that randomly picks a single 3x3 block and decides whether to cleanly swap two non-fixed cells or aggressively replace a non-fixed cell with a random number (1-9).
    SudokuProblem
    Implementation of the Sudoku problem for the Genetic Algorithm.
    SudokuSwapMutation
    Description: Targets a random 3x3 block and swaps two non-fixed cells within it.
    SwapMutation<T>
    Description: Randomly picks two unique gene indices across the chromosome sequence and swaps their contents.
    SymbolicRegressionData
    Data container for Symbolic Regression representing a set of features (x0...xn) and a corresponding target value (y).
    SymRegAddNode
     
    SymRegConstantNode
    Constant node for Symbolic Regression trees.
    SymRegCosNode
     
    SymRegDataPoint
    Represents a data point for symbolic regression.
    SymRegDivideNode
    A division node in a Symbolic Regression expression tree.
    SymRegFitnessFunction
    Symbolic Regression Fitness Function evaluating Abstract Syntax Trees (ASTs) against empirical data points.
    SymRegGAConfig
     
    SymRegMulNode
     
    SymRegNode
    Interface representing a node in a Symbolic Regression expression tree.
    SymRegProblem
    Implementation of the Symbolic Regression problem for the Genetic Algorithm.
    SymRegSinNode
     
    SymRegSubNode
     
    SymRegTreeGenerator
     
    SymRegVariableNode
     
    TournamentSelection<T>
    Description: A selection operator that simulates a gladiator tournament.
    TreeHelper
     
    TSPFitnessFunction
    Fitness Function for the Traveling Salesperson Problem (TSP).
    TSPGAConfig
    Configuration class for setting up a genetic algorithm (GA) to solve the Traveling Salesman Problem (TSP).
    TSPProblem
    Implementation of the Traveling Salesperson Problem for the Genetic Algorithm.
    TwoPointCrossover<T>
    Description: Randomly selects two division points along the genome length.
    UniformCrossover<T>
    Description: Iterates through each gene index and tosses a coin (50% probability) to decide which parent provides the gene to which child.
    UniformMutation<T>
    Description: Iterates through each gene in the chromosome.
